body:
Hey Brittany, just wanted to check in on where we stand with the community outreach initiative. Last I heard we were still waiting on venue confirmations — has that come through yet? I need to give the rest of the committee a status update by Thursday and want to make sure I have accurate info.

Let me know what you've got.

Diana
snippet: Hey Brittany, just wanted to check in on where we stand with the community outreach...
subject: Project update

body:
Hi Diana, good timing — I heard back from two of the three venues this morning. The community center confirmed and the library is still pending but said they'd have an answer by end of week. I'd say we're about 70% on track for the presentation date, though the design materials are running a little behind.

I'm following up with the design lead today. I'll send a more complete update once I have everything in hand.

Brittany
snippet: Hi Diana, good timing — I heard back from two of the three venues this morning. ...
subject: Re: Project update

body:
Hi Brittany, here's my draft agenda for Thursday's team meeting:
1. Follow-up on last week's action items
2. Budget update for Q3 events
3. Volunteer recruitment strategy
4. Open floor

Does anything need to be added or shuffled around? I want to get it out to the full group today if possible.

Diana
snippet:
Hi Brittany, here's my draft agenda for Thursday's team meeting:
1. Follow-up on la...
subject: Team meeting Thursday

body:
Hey Diana, the agenda looks solid. One thing I'd add is a quick item on the social media calendar — we've had some overlap lately and Thursday would be a good time to get everyone aligned on who's posting what and when.

Also maybe cap the budget discussion at 15 minutes so we leave enough room for the recruitment strategy? That one tends to need more time than we give it.

Brittany
snippet: Hey Diana, the agenda looks solid. One thing I'd add is a quick item on the soci...
subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday

body:
Good call on the social media item — I'll slot it in right after the budget update. And yes, 15 minutes is the right call for budget, we always spiral on that one.

I'll send the updated agenda to the group this afternoon. See you Thursday!

Diana
snippet: Good call on the social media item — I'll slot it in right after the budget upda...
subject: Re: Team meeting Thursday

body:
Brittany, I noticed your 1:1 with Denise is coming up next week. Have you started putting anything together for it? These conversations go a lot better when you walk in with your own agenda — wins from the quarter, questions about direction, anything you want visibility on. Don't just wait for her to drive the whole thing.

Let me know if you want to talk it through.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, I noticed your 1:1 with Denise is coming up next week. Have you started p...
subject: Manager 1:1 prep

body:
Honestly I've been a bit anxious about it. I know Q2 feedback is going to come up and I'm not totally sure what to expect. I've been meaning to sit down and write out some notes but I keep putting it off.

What do you think I should make sure to cover? I don't want to look underprepared but I also don't want to come in with a ten-page outline and seem like I'm overcompensating.

Brittany
snippet: Honestly I've been a bit anxious about it. I know Q2 feedback is going to come u...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body:
Lead with your accomplishments — two or three concrete things you delivered this quarter and what the impact was. Don't wait for her to bring them up. Then have a couple of questions ready that show you're thinking about your own growth and where the team is headed.

On feedback, go in with an open mind. If something catches you off guard, it's okay to say 'that's useful, let me sit with it.' You don't have to have a response to everything in the room.

Dad
snippet: Lead with your accomplishments — two or three concrete things you delivered this...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body:
That's really helpful, thank you. I'm going to write out my Q2 highlights tonight and draft a few questions about the roadmap for the rest of the year. I feel a lot better going in with a structure rather than just winging it.

I'll let you know how it goes.

Brittany
snippet: That's really helpful, thank you. I'm going to write out my Q2 highlights tonigh...
subject: Re: Manager 1:1 prep

body:
Brittany, the mandatory compliance training deadline is coming up at the end of the month. I know it slipped by a few people last year and it caused a whole mess with HR. Make sure you carve out time to get it done — the link should be in your inbox from the L&D team. It's about two hours total.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, the mandatory compliance training deadline is coming up at the end of the...
subject: Training reminder

body:
Thanks for the nudge — I did see that email but kept pushing it off. I'll block some time this week. Is there a particular module that takes the longest? I want to budget my time properly so I'm not getting cut off halfway through.

Brittany
snippet: Thanks for the nudge — I did see that email but kept pushing it off. I'll block ...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body:
The data privacy section takes longer than you'd expect — there are scenario questions at the end that require actual reading. The workplace safety module is quick, maybe 20 minutes. And don't start it right before a meeting: you can't pause mid-module without losing your progress on that section.

Also, the deadline is the 30th, not the 31st. Someone learned that the hard way last cycle.

Dad
snippet: The data privacy section takes longer than you'd expect — there are scenario que...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body:
Good to know on the deadline — I'll aim to finish by the 28th to give myself a buffer. I blocked Wednesday afternoon for it, should be about three hours of quiet time. I'll tackle data privacy first while I'm still fresh.

Brittany
snippet: Good to know on the deadline — I'll aim to finish by the 28th to give myself a b...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body:
Wednesday is a smart choice. One more thing — make sure you're on a stable internet connection. The system times out on a weak signal and you have to restart the whole module. Ask me how I know.

Good luck, it's not too painful once you get into it.

Dad
snippet: Wednesday is a smart choice. One more thing — make sure you're on a stable inter...
subject: Re: Training reminder

body:
Hey babe, are you free for lunch any day this week? I've been craving that Thai place on Elm Street and it's been way too long since we've done a proper sit-down in the middle of the day. Let me know what works and I'll move things around on my end.

Tom
snippet: Hey babe, are you free for lunch any day this week? I've been craving that Thai ...
subject: Lunch this week?

body:
Yes, let's do it. Thursday works best — I have a light morning and nothing until 2pm. That Thai place sounds perfect, I've been thinking about their curry noodles for weeks. Should we say 12:30? I can meet you there.

Brittany
snippet: Yes, let's do it. Thursday works best — I have a light morning and nothing until...
subject: Re: Lunch this week?

body:
Hey Brittany, I know things have been hectic lately but I was thinking it'd be nice to actually sit down and catch up properly. Are you free next Tuesday morning? There's that new place on Grant Ave I've been wanting to try — even just an hour would be great.

Tom
snippet: Hey Brittany, I know things have been hectic lately but I was thinking it'd be nice...
subject: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
I'm in — I've walked by that place twice and been tempted both times. Tuesday morning works, I'm free until around 10:30. Would 8:30 give us enough time, or is that too early for you?

Brittany
snippet: I'm in — I've walked by that place twice and been tempted both times. Tuesday mo...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
8:30 is perfect. I'll try to get there a few minutes early and grab a table by the window if I can — that side of the place looks great from the street. See you Tuesday!

Tom
snippet: 8:30 is perfect. I'll try to get there a few minutes early and grab a table by t...
subject: Re: Coffee next Tuesday?

body:
Okay, your birthday is coming up and I want to plan something fun — are you thinking just the two of us or a small group? I've been looking at a couple of spots downtown but I want to know what vibe you're going for before I get too far into planning mode.

Tom
snippet: Okay, your birthday is coming up and I want to plan something fun — are you thin...
subject: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I'm leaning toward something a little more intimate this year — maybe five or six people max. I don't want it to turn into a big production. Somewhere with a solid brunch menu and ideally bottomless mimosas would be perfect. What spots were you looking at? I'm open as long as it's not somewhere deafeningly loud.

Brittany
snippet: I'm leaning toward something a little more intimate this year — maybe five or si...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
I was looking at two places — Clover & Co., which has a really cozy setup and a great seasonal menu, and the rooftop at The Harper, which has an amazing view but can get pretty noisy on weekends. Based on what you said, Clover & Co. sounds more like you.

I can call and check on reservations for a small group. Want me to aim for the Saturday closest to your actual birthday?

Tom
snippet: I was looking at two places — Clover & Co., which has a really cozy setup and a ...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Yes, Clover & Co. sounds perfect — I've heard good things. The Saturday before works better for the people I'd want to invite. Go ahead and make the reservation and let me know if you need me to send you the guest list.

Thank you for planning this, seriously.

Brittany
snippet: Yes, Clover & Co. sounds perfect — I've heard good things. The Saturday before w...
subject: Re: Birthday brunch plans

body:
Brittany, the contract with Hartwell Services is up for renewal at the end of next month. I've been reviewing the current terms and I think we should use this as an opportunity to renegotiate — the response time guarantees in particular are outdated compared to what other vendors are offering now. Have you looked at the agreement recently?

Dad
snippet: Brittany, the contract with Hartwell Services is up for renewal at the end of next ...
subject: Renewal options

body:
I pulled it up yesterday and I agree — the SLA terms are behind market. I also noticed the pricing hasn't been revisited since 2022, which seems like an easy opening. Are we looking to push back on both pricing and service terms, or lead with one and let the other follow?

Brittany
snippet: I pulled it up yesterday and I agree — the SLA terms are behind market. I also n...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Lead with the SLA terms — it keeps the conversation focused on performance rather than just cost, and once we've established that their current terms don't meet our needs, the pricing discussion becomes more natural. I've already reached out to two alternatives quietly, just to have some leverage. Nothing serious, but it helps to have options.

Dad
snippet: Lead with the SLA terms — it keeps the conversation focused on performance rathe...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Smart move lining up alternatives. I can put together a comparison document by end of this week — current terms versus what we'd like to see, with a quick benchmark from the market. That should give us a solid position going into any negotiation. Should I set up a call with their account rep, or do you want to handle that?

Brittany
snippet: Smart move lining up alternatives. I can put together a comparison document by e...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Get the comparison doc done first — I want to review it before we get on a call with them. Once we're aligned on what we're actually asking for, go ahead and schedule it and copy me on the invite.

Good thinking on the benchmarking piece.

Dad
snippet: Get the comparison doc done first — I want to review it before we get on a call ...
subject: Re: Renewal options

body:
Brittany, I'm looking at the latest invoice from Meridian Supplies and the total is about $340 higher than the original PO. Do you have any context on what those additional charges are for? I want to get this sorted before the payment goes out on Friday.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, I'm looking at the latest invoice from Meridian Supplies and the total is...
subject: Vendor invoice question

body:
I looked into it — the difference is a rush shipping charge that was added when we moved up the delivery date last month. I have the email authorizing that change if you need it for the records. It was approved at the time but was never reflected in an updated PO.

I can put together an amended PO to capture it and send it over for your sign-off before we release payment.

Brittany
snippet: I looked into it — the difference is a rush shipping charge that was added when ...
subject: Re: Vendor invoice question

body:
Brittany, your lease is coming up soon if I remember right. Have you heard from the landlord yet about renewal terms? Given where rents are trending in your area, I'd want to know what they're proposing before you commit to anything. Don't just sign whatever they send without reading it carefully.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, your lease is coming up soon if I remember right. Have you heard from the...
subject: Apartment lease renewal

body:
They sent a letter last week — they're proposing a 6% increase, which would put me at $1,680 a month. I looked at a few comparable units in the neighborhood and it seems a bit high, but not outrageous. I haven't responded yet.

I'm also wondering whether it makes more sense to move somewhere with a second bedroom since I've been working from home so much. What do you think I should be weighing here?

Brittany
snippet: They sent a letter last week — they're proposing a 6% increase, which would put ...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
6% is on the high end — I'd push back and see if they'll settle at 3-4%. Landlords generally prefer keeping a reliable tenant over the hassle of finding someone new, so you have more leverage than you think.

On the two-bedroom question, run the actual numbers before you decide. A dedicated workspace has real value if you're home most days, but make sure the cost difference justifies it rather than just assuming it will. Don't let 'nice to have' turn into 'need to have' without the math behind it.

Dad
snippet: 6% is on the high end — I'd push back and see if they'll settle at 3-4%. Landlor...
subject: Re: Apartment lease renewal

body:
Brittany, a couple of things from my reading this week that I thought might be useful for you:

There's a piece in HBR on how managers at your level can build influence without formal authority — very relevant for where you are right now. There's also a short essay on negotiation that's practical rather than theoretical. I'll send the links if you want them.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, a couple of things from my reading this week that I thought might be usef...
subject: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Yes, please send both. The HBR piece especially — I've been thinking a lot about how to have more of a voice in decisions without stepping on toes, particularly with some of the team dynamics shifting lately. The negotiation one also sounds timely given the vendor renewal conversations we've been having.

Brittany
snippet: Yes, please send both. The HBR piece especially — I've been thinking a lot about...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Sending now. The HBR article is paywalled but you should get one free article per month — it's worth using it on this one. The negotiation essay is from the Farnam Street newsletter, you can find it on their site.

The HBR piece has a framework around 'earned credibility versus positional authority' that I think you'll find maps directly to what you're navigating right now.

Dad
snippet: Sending now. The HBR article is paywalled but you should get one free article pe...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Read the HBR piece over lunch — you were right, it's directly applicable. The section on making small wins consistently visible without it feeling like self-promotion is exactly what I needed to read. I'm going to be more intentional about that.

Reading the negotiation essay tonight. Thanks for always passing these along.

Brittany
snippet: Read the HBR piece over lunch — you were right, it's directly applicable. The se...
subject: Re: Newsletter — this week's reads

body:
Brittany, what does the pickup schedule look like this week? I can do Monday and Wednesday without any issues. Tuesday I have a doctor's appointment at 3:30 so I'll need you or someone else to cover that one. Just let me know the plan so I can arrange my day.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, what does the pickup schedule look like this week? I can do Monday and We...
subject: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
I can cover Tuesday myself — I'll leave work a bit early. Monday and Wednesday are all yours, pickup is at 3:15 from Birchwood. Are you also able to do Thursday this week? I have a meeting that might run long and I don't want to cut it close.

Brittany
snippet: I can cover Tuesday myself — I'll leave work a bit early. Monday and Wednesday a...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Thursday works for me too. So I've got Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday — that leaves Tuesday and Friday for you.

Also, Zoe mentioned to me that she wants to join the after-school art program starting next month. You might want to look into registration before spots fill up.

Dad
snippet: Thursday works for me too. So I've got Monday, Wednesday, and Thursday — that le...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Perfect, Tuesday and Friday work for me. Thanks for covering so much this week.

Zoe mentioned the art program to me too! Do you know if it's through the school or an outside organization? I want to make sure I find the right sign-up page and don't miss the deadline.

Brittany
snippet:
Perfect, Tuesday and Friday work for me. Thanks for covering so much this week.
...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
I believe it's through the school's enrichment program — the main office should have the forms. I'd call rather than email, they're much faster on the phone.

Schedule is set then. I'll see the kids Monday. Tell them Grandpa's bringing snacks.

Dad
snippet: I believe it's through the school's enrichment program — the main office should ...
subject: Re: Schedule check — kids pickup

body:
Brittany, I've been putting together some notes on the August trip. We're looking at flying into Charlotte on the 8th and driving up from there to your aunt and uncle's place. They're flexible on exact dates but need at least two weeks' notice to arrange things on their end.

How many days can you realistically take off? That'll help me nail down the itinerary.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, I've been putting together some notes on the August trip. We're looking a...
subject: Family vacation planning

body:
I can take up to five business days in August — I was thinking the 8th through the 14th would work well and lines up with what you're describing. I just need to submit the time-off request by end of the month, so the sooner we finalize the better.

Are we planning to stay at their place the whole time, or is there a point where we'd split off somewhere? I want to make sure we're thinking about the kids' comfort for longer stretches.

Brittany
snippet: I can take up to five business days in August — I was thinking the 8th through t...
subject: Re: Family vacation planning

body:
Brittany, do you have anything going on this weekend? Your mother and I were thinking of having everyone over Sunday for a cookout — nothing formal, just the family. Let us know if you and the kids can make it.

Dad
snippet: Brittany, do you have anything going on this weekend? Your mother and I were thinki...
subject: Weekend plans

body:
Sunday works great, we'd love to come. The kids have been asking about grandma's potato salad so the timing is perfect. What time should we plan to get there, and is there anything you need me to bring?

Brittany
snippet: Sunday works great, we'd love to come. The kids have been asking about grandma's...
subject: Re: Weekend plans

body:
We'll get started around 1pm — come whenever you're ready, no need to be on the dot. Just bring yourselves, your mother bought enough food to feed the entire street. See you Sunday!

Dad
snippet: We'll get started around 1pm — come whenever you're ready, no need to be on the ...
